name: Tathlum Stone
description: The Tathlum Stone is a magical stone of immense power appearing in Irish mythology, particularly associated with the Fomorians and figures like Balor of the Evil Eye. It is described as a large, heavy stone capable of being hurled with devastating force and accuracy. Its origins are somewhat mysterious, but it's generally believed to be a weapon of the Fomorians, representing their destructive capabilities and connection to the primordial forces of chaos. The stone is not merely a projectile; it seems to possess a malevolent intelligence or magical charge, enhancing its destructive power. Some accounts suggest the stone is imbued with the essence of Balor's evil eye, making it an extension of his destructive gaze. The impact of the Tathlum Stone is said to cause widespread destruction, leaving craters and devastation in its wake. It is a symbol of brute force and the overwhelming power of the Fomorian adversaries faced by the Tuatha Dé Danann. The rarity of its appearance in the myths suggests it was a weapon of last resort, reserved for moments of critical conflict. The stone's weight and the strength required to wield it further emphasize the formidable nature of those who possessed it. Its significance lies in representing the constant threat posed by the Fomorians and the challenges faced by the Tuatha Dé Danann in maintaining order and balance in the world. The Tathlum Stone embodies the raw, untamed power of the ancient world, a power that could easily shatter the fragile peace established by the gods.
subtitle: A weapon of devastating Fomorian power
origin: Forged or found by the Fomorians, possibly imbued with dark magic.
currentLocation: Unknown, possibly lost or hidden in the depths of the sea or within Fomorian strongholds.

keyMyths.title: Battle of Mag Tuired
keyMyths.description: The Tathlum Stone is likely used by the Fomorians during the Second Battle of Mag Tuired, contributing to the chaos and destruction of the conflict. It represents the immense power wielded by the Fomorian forces.
keyMyths.source: The Second Battle of Mag Tuired

keyMyths.title: Balor's Weapon
keyMyths.description: The stone is seen as an extension of Balor's power, a physical manifestation of his destructive gaze, capable of causing widespread devastation upon impact. This makes it a terrifying weapon in his arsenal.

sources.author: Jeffrey Gantz
sources.description: A compilation of early Irish texts, including those related to the Mythological Cycle.
sources.text: A compilation of early Irish texts, including those related to the Mythological Cycle.

sources.author: Peter Berresford Ellis
